narrative: Sunday morning, clear and cold, the snow already pulling back from the south-facing slopes. A jogger on the Rimrocks trail spotted a mountain lion at dawn — or says she did, crouched on a ledge above the sandstone, watching her with the patient stillness of something that doesn’t need to hurry. Fish & Wildlife closes the trail by eight and the news travels through Billings the way it always does: fast, half-accurate, growing with each retelling. By noon it’ll be two mountain lions and a near-attack. Amber is at the bookstore meeting the insurance adjuster, a man named Phil who takes photographs of water damage with the enthusiasm of someone who has done this eight hundred times and will do it eight hundred more. Cole is up early, coffee in the dark, and the dog is on the porch. Not inside. But on the porch. Nora is back at St. Vincent for the Sunday morning shift, which is always the shift where Friday night’s bad decisions arrive as Sunday morning’s emergencies.
events: Someone reports a mountain lion sighting near the Rimrocks trailhead — Fish & Wildlife closes the trail
Amber opens Tuck’s Books early to meet the insurance adjuster about the pipe damage
